notes.content: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.
notes.contentHtml:
All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website.


The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

notes.content: THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the " Transfer Admission Screening Policy " below. Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion. For more information, including a list of screening majors and non-screening majors , visit the UCSC Admissions website.
notes.contentHtml:
THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the "Transfer Admission Screening Policy" below.


Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion.


For more information, including a list of screening majors and non-screening majors, visit the UCSC Admissions website.

notes.content: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .
notes.contentHtml: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website.

notes.content: Transfer Admission Screening Policy Transfer applicants must complete the articulated equivalents of at least FIVE courses listed under the “Required Transfer Admission Screening Courses” section below, earning a grade of C (2.0) or better in each course, by the end of the spring term prior to starting at UCSC in the fall. Strongly Recommended Major Preparation Courses In addition to the courses required for admission to this major, prospective transfer students are strongly encouraged to complete the articulated equivalents of the "Recommended Major Preparation Courses" and "Additional Major Preparation" courses listed below . These courses serve as prerequisites for upper-division requirements, and students who do not complete them prior to transfer may need additional quarters to finish their degree. Students should complete at least two courses from the "Additional Major Preparation Courses" section most relevant to their interests: Earth Sciences, Electrical and Computer Engineering, or Physics (Section A) Computer Science and Engineering or Electrical and Computer Engineering (Section B) Economics or Statistics (Section C) Mathematical Biology (Section D) Students are also strongly encouraged to complete a programming course in Python or C before transferring. If unavailable, a course in another programming language is acceptable to gain familiarity with general programming concepts and environments. Additional Information for Transfer Students are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or as many UC Santa Cruz general education requirements as possible before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor. Visit the Baskin Engineering Undergraduate Experience website to learn more about this major. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Applied Mathematics B.S. Program Statement
notes.contentHtml:
Transfer Admission Screening Policy

Transfer applicants must complete the articulated equivalents of at least FIVE courses listed under the “Required Transfer Admission Screening Courses” section below, earning a grade of C (2.0) or better in each course, by the end of the spring term prior to starting at UCSC in the fall.



Strongly Recommended Major Preparation Courses

In addition to the courses required for admission to this major, prospective transfer students are strongly encouraged to complete the articulated equivalents of the "Recommended Major Preparation Courses" and "Additional Major Preparation" courses listed below. These courses serve as prerequisites for upper-division requirements, and students who do not complete them prior to transfer may need additional quarters to finish their degree.


Students should complete at least two courses from the "Additional Major Preparation Courses" section most relevant to their interests:


  • Earth Sciences, Electrical and Computer Engineering, or Physics (Section A)
  • Computer Science and Engineering or Electrical and Computer Engineering (Section B)
  • Economics or Statistics (Section C)
  • Mathematical Biology (Section D)


Students are also strongly encouraged to complete a programming course in Python or C before transferring. If unavailable, a course in another programming language is acceptable to gain familiarity with general programming concepts and environments.



Additional Information for Transfer

Students are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or as many UC Santa Cruz general education requirements as possible before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.



Visit the Baskin Engineering Undergraduate Experience website to learn more about this major.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Applied Mathematics B.S. Program Statement
